metadata.name: Kubernetes ClusterRoleBinding
metadata.description: Creates a Kubernetes ClusterRoleBinding that binds one subject to a Role or ClusterRole.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Creates a Kubernetes ClusterRoleBinding resource for cluster-scoped RBAC access.

## Prerequisites
- The referenced Role or ClusterRole must already exist.
- The target subject should already exist, especially for ServiceAccount subjects.

## Apply
- Render `main.yaml` and review the binding carefully because this resource can grant cluster-wide access.
- Apply the manifest only after confirming the selected role matches the least-privilege access you actually need.

## Follow-Up
- Validate the result with `kubectl auth can-i` or by testing the bound workload directly.

## References
- [RBAC Documentation](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/rbac/)

variables.title: Referenced role name
variables.description: Name to use for the referenced role.

variables.description: Name to use for the subject.
